landing.heroTitle: Compassionate Care for Your Loved Ones
landing.heroSubtitle: Professional home health and hospice services dedicated to providing quality care with dignity and respect.
landing.philosophyText: We believe in treating our patients with dignity, respect, and empathy. We strive to make their end-of-life journey as comfortable and meaningful as possible.
landing.caringSinceText: Providing compassionate care in the comfort of your home since 2021.
landing.aboutText: Compassionate Home Health & Hospice is a family-owned and operated business founded by Jane Smith, RN. With over 15 years of experience in healthcare, Jane started this company to provide personalized, compassionate care to patients in their homes. Our team of dedicated professionals is committed to enhancing the quality of life for our patients and their families through comprehensive home health and hospice services.

homeHealth.title: Home Health
homeHealth.subtitle: Professional healthcare services delivered in the comfort and safety of your home, promoting independence and quality of life.

services.doctorsDesc: Our MDs develop and review care plans, manage symptoms, and act as a medical resource.
services.ptDesc: Help patients maintain mobility, manage pain, and improve quality of life.
services.otDesc: Assist patients in maintaining independence in daily activities.
services.nursesDesc: Collaborate with physicians and provide medical care at home.
services.stDesc: Help patients improve communication, swallowing, and cognitive function.
services.chhaDesc: Assist with daily activities like bathing, dressing, and meal prep.
services.mswDesc: Provide emotional support and connect patients with community resources.
services.multilingualDesc: Services available in multiple languages to support diverse patient needs.

blurb.title: Hospice Care
blurb.description: Providing comfort, dignity, and compassionate support during life's final journey. We're here for patients and their families every step of the way.

card1.title: What is Hospice
card1.description: Hospice care focuses on providing comfort, quality of life, and support for people nearing the end of life, rather than curing the illness. It is provided by a team of professionals who manage pain and other symptoms, and address emotional, physical, spiritual, and social needs of both the patient and their family through passion. Hospice care can be provided in a person's home, a dedicated hospice facility, and the goal is to help the person live their remaining time with dignity and as peacefully as possible.

card2.title: Who is eligible
card2.description: No matter your faith or beliefs, you and your family can receive spiritual and emotional support from our care team.

card3.title: How is hospice different?
card3.description: Our team provides a compassionate environment for patients and families, so they can feel supported and understood during end-of-life care

card4.title: Why might I choose?
card4.description: Our hospice team provides emotional and spiritual support to families as they navigate end-of-life care.

card5.title: Who provides the care?
card5.description: The foundation of our service is a team of highly skilled and trained professionals providing compassionate and personalized care tailored to the unique needs of each individual. We have a disciplinary team of various professionals, including physicians, nurses, social workers, chaplains, and hospice aides, to provide comprehensive care and support to patients and their families. Other disciplines like physical, occupational, and speech therapists, as well as volunteers and bereavement specialists, may also be involved, depending on the patient's individualized specific needs and the hospice provider's orders.

card1.title: What does hospice provide?
card1.description: Hospice offers care from a team of different types of practioners who work together to meet the medical, psychological and spiritual needs of you and your family.

card2.title: Hours avail when in hospice care...
card2.description: Hospice provides you a phone number to call 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, to help answer any questions you may have.

card3.title: What other benefits do patients have when they are under hospicecare

description.top: Once you are admitted into the Hospice Program, you will be provided with:
description.list3: - medications for symptoms and pain relief

card4.title: Physician
card4.description: Your personal physician continues to provide your care while you're in the Hospice Program. A hospice physician works closely with your personal physician and can consult with you.

card5.title: Nurse
card5.description: A nurse visits during the week to instruct your caregiver and to coordinate your care. A nurse informs the hospice physician of your condition on a regular basis.

description.top: - A social worker helps you and your caregiver by:
description.list2: - assisting with practical matters, such as finding additional help in the home

card1.title: Hospiceaide/homemaker services
card1.description: A hospice aide visits during the week as needed to help you and your caregiver with personal care such as changing linens, giving you a bath and helping you get dressed.

card2.title: Chaplain
card2.description: A chaplain is a minister or counselor dedicated to meeting the spiritual needs of you and your family, no matter your religion. You and your family can ask for the chaplain anytime.

card3.title: Volunteer
card3.description: A volunteer is someone from the community who has been trained by our Hospice Program to help you and your family. Volunteers provide services under the direction of a hospice staff member.

card4.title: What about medical appointments?
card4.description: Your personal physician is in charge of your care, even after you're admitted to our Hospice Program. A hospice physician will work with your personal physician to provide the care your need.

card5.title: What kind of help might I need?
card5.description: At some point, you may become confined to bed and unable to turn over or eat without help. Your caregiver may need to change your diapers and linens and care for your skin to prevent bedsores. Hospice nurses will teach caregivers how to perform these tasks.

card6.title: What if a difficult situation arises?
card6.description: We understand that caregivers may face difficult situations. A 24-hour phone line puts the caregiver in touch with a hospice-trained nurse who can give advice over the phone or make a home visit if necessary.

aboveAndBeyond.title: Above and Beyond Care
aboveAndBeyond.description: We understand that hospice care affects the entire family. Our approach goes beyond medical care to provide emotional, spiritual, and practical support for everyone involved. From the initial consultation through bereavement, we walk alongside you with understanding and care.

card1.title: Referral
card1.description: Doctor, Hospital, or family starts the process.

card2.title: Assessment
card2.description: Nurse or therapist evaluates health, abilities, and family goals.

card3.title: Care Plan
card3.description: Customized plan including nursing, therapy, and supporting services.

card4.title: Ongoing Support
card4.description: Regular visits, telehealth check-ins, and caregiver guidance.

card5.title: Communication
card5.description: Open dialogue between patient, family, and care team.

hospiceContact.title: We're Here to Help
hospiceContact.description: Our compassionate hospice team is available 24/7 to answer your questions and provide support.
